title: Homity Pie

overview.description: A traditional British vegetarian pie filled with potatoes, onions, mature Cheddar and fresh parsley in a crisp shortcrust pastry case.

method: Line a 23cm pie dish with the shortcrust pastry.
Blind bake at 180°C fan (200°C conventional) for 15 minutes.
Boil the potatoes in salted water for 10–12 minutes until just tender.
Meanwhile, melt the butter in a frying pan over a medium heat.
Cook the onions for 10–12 minutes until soft and lightly golden.
Combine the potatoes, onions, Cheddar, parsley, milk and mustard, if using.
Season generously with salt and black pepper.
Spoon the filling into the pastry case.
Bake at 180°C fan (200°C conventional) for 30–35 minutes until golden and bubbling.
Allow to stand for 10 minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Homity Pie became popular in Britain during the Second World War as a filling vegetarian meal that relied on simple home-grown ingredients.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to blind bake pastry, balance a simple savoury filling and bake a pie with a crisp base.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A classic British comfort food that turns simple pantry ingredients into a satisfying vegetarian meal.
chapman-tips: Allow the onions to soften slowly for the best flavour.
Use a strong mature Cheddar, as it's the main flavour of the pie.
Don't overcook the potatoes before baking—they'll continue cooking in the oven.
Blind baking helps prevent a soggy pastry base.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze either before or after baking.
notes.leftovers: Excellent cold for lunch or reheated in the oven.
